title: ABCDE APPROACH
content: Initial evaluation of unstable patients follows ABCDE sequence. Airway. Breathing. Circulation. Disability. Exposure.

quiz.q: What is the first step when assessing an unstable patient?

title: AIRWAY OBSTRUCTION
content: Signs include inability to speak, stridor, cyanosis. Immediate airway management required.

quiz.q: What is the most urgent step in airway obstruction?

title: MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION
content: Typical presentation includes chest pressure, radiation to arm or jaw, sweating and nausea.

quiz.q: What is the first diagnostic test in suspected MI?

title: STEMI MANAGEMENT
content: ST elevation myocardial infarction requires urgent reperfusion therapy.

quiz.q: What is the definitive treatment for STEMI?

title: PNEUMOTHORAX
content: Air in pleural space causing lung collapse. Symptoms include sudden dyspnea and chest pain.

quiz.q: What physical finding suggests pneumothorax?

title: TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX
content: Life threatening condition with tracheal deviation, hypotension, severe dyspnea.

quiz.q: What is the immediate treatment?

title: STROKE
content: Sudden neurological deficit such as weakness, speech difficulty, vision loss.

quiz.q: What imaging is first used in stroke?

title: INTRACRANIAL HEMORRHAGE
content: Bleeding inside the skull causing increased intracranial pressure.

quiz.q: What symptom strongly suggests intracranial bleeding?

title: ASTHMA EXACERBATION
content: Acute worsening of asthma with wheezing and shortness of breath.

quiz.q: What medication relieves acute asthma symptoms?

title: COPD
content: Chronic airflow limitation usually due to smoking.

quiz.q: What is the main risk factor for COPD?

title: UPPER GI BLEED
content: Bleeding from esophagus stomach or duodenum.

quiz.q: What symptom suggests upper GI bleeding?

title: PEPTIC ULCER
content: Ulceration of stomach or duodenum often caused by H pylori or NSAIDs.

quiz.q: What bacterium causes many peptic ulcers?

title: ACUTE KIDNEY INJURY
content: Sudden decline in kidney function leading to rise in creatinine.

quiz.q: What lab marker rises in acute kidney injury?

title: URINARY TRACT INFECTION
content: Bacterial infection of urinary tract with dysuria and frequency.

quiz.q: What symptom strongly suggests UTI?

title: DIABETES MELLITUS
content: Chronic hyperglycemia due to insulin deficiency or resistance.

quiz.q: What hormone regulates blood glucose?

title: HYPERTHYROIDISM
content: Excess thyroid hormone causing weight loss heat intolerance and tachycardia.

quiz.q: What symptom suggests hyperthyroidism?

title: SEPSIS
content: Systemic inflammatory response due to infection causing organ dysfunction.

quiz.q: What treatment should be given early in sepsis?

title: ANEMIA
content: Reduction in hemoglobin leading to fatigue pallor and shortness of breath.

quiz.q: What symptom commonly occurs in anemia?

title: DEPRESSION
content: Persistent low mood loss of interest sleep and appetite changes.

quiz.q: What term describes loss of pleasure?

title: ANAPHYLAXIS
content: Severe allergic reaction with airway swelling hypotension and rash.

quiz.q: What is the first line treatment for anaphylaxis?

title: SUBARACHNOID HEMORRHAGE
content: Sudden severe headache described as worst headache of life. May occur with neck stiffness and vomiting. Often caused by ruptured aneurysm.

quiz.q: What symptom description strongly suggests subarachnoid hemorrhage?

title: EPIDURAL HEMATOMA
content: Head trauma followed by brief lucid interval then neurological deterioration. Often due to rupture of middle meningeal artery.

quiz.q: What classic history occurs in epidural hematoma?

title: SUBDURAL HEMATOMA
content: Bleeding from bridging veins usually after minor trauma especially in elderly. Symptoms may develop slowly.

quiz.q: What group commonly develops subdural hematoma?

title: ATRIAL FIBRILLATION
content: Irregular atrial rhythm causing irregularly irregular pulse and absence of organized P waves.

quiz.q: What pulse finding suggests atrial fibrillation?

title: SUPRAVENTRICULAR TACHYCARDIA
content: Sudden onset narrow complex tachycardia often causing palpitations and dizziness.

quiz.q: What maneuver may terminate supraventricular tachycardia?

title: PULMONARY EMBOLISM
content: Obstruction of pulmonary arteries by clot. Symptoms include sudden dyspnea chest pain tachycardia.

quiz.q: What symptom strongly suggests pulmonary embolism?

title: PNEUMONIA
content: Infection of lung tissue causing fever cough and shortness of breath.

quiz.q: What imaging confirms pneumonia?

title: APPENDICITIS
content: Inflammation of appendix with pain that starts around umbilicus then moves to right lower abdomen.

quiz.q: Where does appendicitis pain localize later?

title: PANCREATITIS
content: Inflammation of pancreas causing severe epigastric pain radiating to the back.

quiz.q: What laboratory enzyme rises in pancreatitis?

title: CHOLECYSTITIS
content: Inflammation of gallbladder commonly due to gallstones. Causes right upper quadrant pain.

quiz.q: What imaging is first used for suspected gallbladder inflammation?

title: KIDNEY STONES
content: Stone in urinary tract causing severe flank pain radiating to groin.

quiz.q: What classic pain radiation occurs with kidney stones?

title: ACUTE URINARY RETENTION
content: Sudden inability to urinate with painful bladder distension.

quiz.q: What is the immediate treatment?

title: DIABETIC KETOACIDOSIS
content: Severe insulin deficiency causing hyperglycemia ketosis and metabolic acidosis.

quiz.q: What breathing pattern occurs in diabetic ketoacidosis?

title: HYPOGLYCEMIA
content: Low blood glucose causing sweating tremor confusion and possible loss of consciousness.

quiz.q: What is the immediate treatment for severe hypoglycemia?

title: IRON DEFICIENCY ANEMIA
content: Most common anemia worldwide caused by iron deficiency leading to reduced hemoglobin production.

quiz.q: What RBC size occurs in iron deficiency anemia?

title: MENINGITIS
content: Infection of meninges presenting with fever neck stiffness and altered mental status.

quiz.q: What physical sign suggests meningitis?

title: CELLULITIS
content: Bacterial infection of skin causing redness swelling warmth and pain.

quiz.q: What physical findings suggest cellulitis?

title: NECROTIZING FASCIITIS
content: Severe rapidly spreading infection of soft tissue causing extreme pain and tissue destruction.

quiz.q: What clinical clue suggests necrotizing fasciitis?
quiz.a: Pain out of proportion to exam.

title: SEROTONIN SYNDROME
content: Excess serotonin activity causing agitation hyperreflexia and autonomic instability.

quiz.q: What neuromuscular sign suggests serotonin syndrome?

title: SEPTIC SHOCK
content: Severe infection causing circulatory failure and organ dysfunction.

quiz.q: What treatment must be started early in septic shock?
